rulings.Case Title: DAVID W WHEELER VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, March 29, 2010, line 1, DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO DEMURRER TO SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT. Demurrer sustained without leave to amend as to the second cause of action. No statutory basis supports this cause of action. No special relationship exists between plaintiff and defendants. No legal duty is owed to plaintiff for the alleged misconduct. No reasonable probability that defect can be cured by amendment. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: ADRIAN FOX VS. NISSAN NORTH AMERICA, INC

rulings.Case Title: JOSEPH FESTINESE AND MONIQUE MODESTE FESTINESE et al VS. JEAN JUDNICK
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Interlocutory Decree For Appointment Of Referee And Order For Sale Of Realty
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, March 29, 2010, line 3, PLAINTIFFS JOSEPH FESTINESE AND MONIQUE MODESTE FESTINESE, INNOCENTE CASSOLATO, TRUSTEE OF THE INNOCENTE, CAROL CASSOLATO Motion for Interlocutory Decree for Appointment of Referee and Order for Sale of Realty. Motion denied. CCP section 872.710(a) provides at trial the court shall determine whether the plaintiff has a right to partition. This motion is premature. No competent evidence establishes the parties interests. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: MC SQUARED HOLDINGS INC., VS. NSA WIRELESS INC., et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, March 29, 2010, line 4, DEFENDANT NSA WIRELESS, INC DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Demurrer overruled in part and sustained in part. Demurrer overruled as to 1st, 4th, 6th, and 8th causes of action. Whether B&P Code section 7031(a) precludes MC Squared from seeking compensation for services rendered is a factual issue not appropriate for demurrer. Demurrer sustained with ten days leave to amend as to 3rd (unfair competition), 5th (Intentional Misrepresentation), and 7th (Intentional Interference with Prospective Economic Advantage) causes of action. Plaintiff to plead misrepresentation with specificity, and allege what particular practices of defendant are unfair pursuant to B&P Code section 17200. To support a claim of Intentional Interference, plaintiff must allege an existing economic relationship that defendant interfered with. See Roth vs. Rhodes (1994) 25 Cal.App. 4th 530, 546. Demurrer sustained without leave to amend as to 2nd cause of action (Breach of Implied Covenant) which does not give rise to an action in tort except in the context of an insurance contract. Sutherland vs. Barclays American/Mortgage Corp. (1997) 53 Cal.App. 4th 299, 314. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: THE RESORT AT INDIAN SPRINGS, LLC VS. CALIFORNIA DIVISION OF LABOR STANDARDS ENFORCEMENT
rulings.Calendar Matter: (Amended) Notice Of Petitioner'S Motion For Default Jdugemnt And Motion For Order Compelling Respondents Toproduce The Adminstrative Record
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, March 29, 2010, line 5, PETITIONER THE RESORT AT INDIAN SPRINGS, LLC (Amended) Notice of Petitioner's Motion for Default Judgment and Motion for Order Compelling Respondents to produce the Adminstrative Record. Motion denied. petitioner fails to establish that Respondent's answer was filed in an untimely manner under CCP section 1089.5. =(302/CWW)
